name: soak-logging
goal: Flag-gated objective per-run usage log baked into opted-in skills via one shared `## Usage Logging` pattern in PATTERNS.md (referenced as a gated terminal step by daily-winddown, project-exit, update-project), plus a soak-review BUILD skill (Tier 2). Content-only markdown; inert when usage_log absent/false.
globalConstraints: Run ENTIRELY in the worktree feature/soak-logging. Never switch branches in the main repo.
~/code/arete-reserv is READ-ONLY during the build. No writes/commits/cp there until the post-merge re-sync gate.
Content-only: NO npm run build, NO dist commit. packages/runtime has no build step; skills ship as raw markdown. The usual commit-dist rule does NOT apply.
Preserve the inert-off invariant: the STOP gate is the literal first sentence of the pattern AND restated in every SKILL.md ref line (R1). No state mutation (not even mkdir) before the gate.
Do not break existing PATTERNS.md references (daily-winddown refs it 9x). Append the new section; do not reorder existing ones.
requiredReading: dev/work/plans/soak-logging/plan.md - full design, entry format, scope tiers
dev/work/plans/soak-logging/review.md - structural verification + Observation A (project-exit Step 7)
packages/runtime/skills/PATTERNS.md - existing ## Section pattern style (do-all-work-then-engage, reconcile-engine)
memory/entries/2026-06-22_winddown-approval-stack-learnings.md - 'fail loud, never silently switch formats' -> why model-tier is mandatory

tasks.title: Add ## Usage Logging pattern to PATTERNS.md
tasks.description: Append a new ## Usage Logging section after the last pattern in packages/runtime/skills/PATTERNS.md, matching existing pattern prose style. The literal first sentence is the hard gate: read usage_log from arete.yaml; if absent or false, STOP - do nothing, create no directory. If true, after the skill's real work append ONE entry to the absolute workspace path <workspace-root>/dev/soak/<skill-id>.md using the labeled format from the plan. Model tier mandatory (model writes its own tier). Every field present each entry; use ' · —' for an empty field rather than dropping the bullet. Pointers to heavy artifacts, never inline bodies.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1.1 The STOP gate is the literal first sentence of the section (R1).
AC1.2 Append path is absolute/workspace-root-anchored, never a bare relative dev/soak/ (R6).

AC1.3 Entry format has all bold labels (Config, Commands, Artifacts, Outcome, Anomalies, Agent notes) + the dated header line with model tier; format note requires every field present, '—' for empty (R4).
AC1.4 No existing pattern reordered; daily-winddown's 9 PATTERNS.md references still resolve.
mitigations: R1: gate first sentence, no mkdir before gate.
R4: all fields present, '—' for empty.
tasks.tests: Manual: grep the new section; confirm gate is first sentence; confirm format block intact.

tasks.title: daily-winddown gated terminal-step ref line
tasks.description: Add a single gated terminal-step line after daily-winddown's always-run report/apply step (Step 6), BEFORE the trailing ## References / ## Rollback blocks (R5). Wording carries the gate: 'After the final report, if usage_log is true in arete.yaml, apply the Usage Logging pattern (PATTERNS.md § Usage Logging); otherwise do nothing.'
acceptanceCriteria: AC2.1 Line carries the gate itself, not only PATTERNS.md (R1).
AC2.2 Placed after the report step, NOT inside ## References / ## Rollback (R5).
mitigations: R5: anchored to always-run report step.
tasks.tests: Manual: locate the line; confirm placement after report step and before References.

tasks.title: project-exit Step 7 (Post-report instrumentation)
tasks.description: Add a new Step 7 (Post-report instrumentation) carrying the gate, so logging survives the silent fast-path (0 durable proposals) which still runs the report step (review Obs A). Same gated wording as T2.
acceptanceCriteria: AC3.1 Step 7 exists, carries the gate (R1).
AC3.2 Step 7 is reached on BOTH the full flow and the silent fast-path.
mitigations: R5/ObsA: Step 7 after report, survives fast-path.
tasks.tests: Manual: confirm Step 7 present and reachable from both flows described in the skill.

tasks.title: update-project gated terminal-step ref line
tasks.description: Add the gated terminal-step line after update-project's Step 5 (Report). Same gated wording as T2.
tasks.tests: Manual: locate the line after Step 5.

tasks.title: Author soak-review BUILD skill (Tier 2)
tasks.description: Author a soak-review skill in the current BUILD harness (.pi/skills/soak-review/). It reads ~/code/arete-reserv/dev/soak/<feature>.md + pointed-at artifacts + arete-reserv git diff since last checkpoint, diffs against the last review checkpoint (new runs only), prompts for a one-line gut reaction per run/cluster, and synthesizes into SOAK-FINDINGS.md grouped by severity with fixes cheapest-first (tier bump -> prompt tighten -> architecture). FIRST STEP: diff the live workspace skill copies (~/code/arete-reserv/.arete/skills/{PATTERNS.md, <skill>/SKILL.md}) against canonical and flag drift before trusting the log (R3). Separable commit from T1-T4.
acceptanceCriteria: AC5.1 First step diffs workspace skill copies vs canonical and flags drift (R3).
AC5.2 Reads across the repo boundary by absolute path; checkpoints to avoid re-reviewing old runs.
AC5.3 Output is severity-grouped findings with cheapest-first fixes; prompts for subjective reaction (the subjective field deliberately omitted from the capture file).
mitigations: R3: workspace-vs-canonical diff as first step.
R7: separable commit; capture (T1-T4) has standalone value if review harness moves.
tasks.tests: Manual dry-run against an existing dev/soak entry once capture has produced one.
